summary: Implement the M6 machine-readable headless CLI with pinned native rendering, named parameter sets, printability checks, and no-window CI export proof.

externalInputs.purpose: Download the exact already-recorded official Windows engine artifact for local acceptance; verify the archive and executable against ENGINE_VERSION before execution. No source code was consulted.

decisions.decision: Dispatch only the exact render, export, params, check, help, and version command set before constructing the Tauri application. Reuse the native-engine crate for immutable project staging, full-quality render/export, typed parameter overrides, engine-version validation, and geometry decoding; emit one JSON object and stable exit codes instead of opening a window.
decisions.rationale: FR-15.12 requires the desktop artifact itself to be automatable without a second renderer or a GUI dependency. Early dispatch preserves one engine authority and makes success and failure machine-readable.

decisions.topic: CLI project boundary and acceptance proof
decisions.decision: Treat the nearest ancestor containing scadmill.project.json as the project root, otherwise use the input file directory; skip symlinks and generated/dependency trees; cap snapshots at 4,096 files and 512 MiB. Run the normative named-set 3MF export explicitly in both Windows and Linux hosted CI against the exact checksummed engine, with DISPLAY removed by the test.
decisions.rationale: Nested entry files must retain their project pin and shared dependencies, while unbounded or link-following scans would create avoidable trust and resource risks. An explicitly invoked real-engine test prevents an absent local engine from masquerading as AC-15.h evidence.

testEvidence.criterion: The desktop artifact must recognize the exact headless command set, parse the normative export syntax, emit JSON, and avoid GUI startup.
testEvidence.test: Compile focused unit and process-level tests for command dispatch, argument parsing, Unicode schema output, machine-readable usage errors, and the named-set destination contract.

red.outcome: Original RED execution time was not retained; compilation failed because run_headless_cli, parse_arguments, CommandKind, schema extraction, set decoding, and export destination behavior did not exist.

green.command: cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml headless_cli::tests -- --nocapture; cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --test headless_cli -- --nocapture; cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --bin scadmill -- --nocapture
green.outcome: GREEN: 6 CLI unit tests passed; process integration passed 2 tests with the exact-engine acceptance test explicitly ignored for its dedicated lane; both executable-dispatch tests passed.

testEvidence.criterion: Nested entry files, dotted output directories, and the Customizer cutoff must preserve their intended project, export, and schema boundaries.
testEvidence.test: Create temporary nested projects with a root manifest and shared source, plus an existing dotted output directory; then assert the collected logical entry, project pin, dependency map, generated 3MF destination, include/use pass-through, and exclusion of assignments after the first geometry statement.

red.command: cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml headless_cli::tests::treats_an_existing_dotted_output_directory_as_a_directory -- --nocapture; cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml headless_cli::tests::uses_the_manifest_directory_as_the_project_root_for_nested_models -- --nocapture; cargo test --locked --manifest-path src/desktop-shell/src-tauri/Cargo.toml headless_cli::tests::extracts_only_top_level_literal_customizer_parameters -- --nocapture
red.outcome: RED: the dotted directory was returned as a file destination; the nested model was incorrectly reduced from models/fixture.scad to fixture.scad with the root dependency omitted; and params leaked the literal late assignment after cube(1) while losing the first parameter after a semicolon-free include.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 6 CLI unit tests passed, including both falsification cases.

testEvidence.criterion: AC-15.h must perform a real full-quality named-set 3MF export with the pinned engine and no display server.
testEvidence.test: Verify the official archive and executable hashes from ENGINE_VERSION, then explicitly invoke the ignored acceptance test with SCADMILL_OPENSCAD set and DISPLAY removed inside the child command.

red.outcome: ENVIRONMENT RED: 25 unit tests passed, then native acceptance rejected the machine's PATH OpenSCAD 2021.01 because 2026.06.12 was required. No product assertion failed.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 real-engine acceptance test passed; the command exited successfully and wrote the expected non-empty ZIP-based fixture-thick.3mf artifact.

testEvidence.criterion: The complete native/desktop slice and synchronized public documentation must remain buildable and policy-clean.
testEvidence.test: Run all native and desktop tests against the verified engine, both Rust clippy gates, TypeScript/type and source-policy checks, public-version checks, Rust license policy, and every website route test/build.

red.outcome: The first environment-bound run exposed only the stale PATH engine described above; this was corrected by selecting verified official bytes, not by changing a test.

green.command: cargo test/clippy for native-engine and desktop-shell; pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd check:public-surfaces; pnpm.cmd check:licenses rust; npm.cmd test --prefix website; npm.cmd run build --prefix website
green.outcome: GREEN: native groups passed 25, 1, 3, 1, and 11 tests; desktop groups passed 71, 2, 2 with 1 explicitly ignored, and 1; the explicit CLI acceptance passed 1; both clippy gates passed; TypeScript passed; lint checked 595 files; 2 public-surface tests, 507-package Rust license policy, and 3 website route tests passed; the website production build completed.

nearMisses.sought: Run native acceptance against the required engine rather than accepting whatever executable appears first on PATH.
nearMisses.confirmation: The acceptance gate rejected OpenSCAD 2021.01. The official 2026.06.12 archive was downloaded once, matched archive SHA-256 3AA51474EA66609FB3FAFA4AA7F2AB4B6FE3FF50C130184F11BBE3818F3EF5AA and executable SHA-256 DE9A0C732C23C3FEB0B49CF938777AA0AEE3E206DB9E98571672CACC4816C524, and only those bytes were executed.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
